lines.text: To recommend that Bureau Liaison contact OSI to determine whether photographs allegedly taken by the captioned individual of a flying disc in 1947 were furnished by OSI to the office of Drew Pearson.

Bureau files reflect that on August 29, 1947, a [REDACTED], called at the Phoenix Office exhibiting credentials reflecting he represented A-2, Fourth Air Force, Hamilton Field, California. He asked an Agent to accompany him to interview [REDACTED] who previously reported the photographing of a flying disc in Phoenix on the afternoon of July 7, 1947.

The Phoenix Office contacted SAC Harry Kimball of the San Francisco Office and Mr. Kimball verified the fact that [REDACTED] came to Phoenix as a representative of Colonel [REDACTED] of Hamilton Field, and it was suggested the Phoenix Office assist [REDACTED] not in investigating the matter, but in simply talking with Dr. [REDACTED].

By letter dated September 4, 1947, the Phoenix Office set forth in four pages the results of this interview and stated [REDACTED] had requested the negatives from [REDACTED] who did not have them in his immediate possession. [REDACTED] stated, however, he would deliver them to the FBI the following morning for transmittal to [REDACTED] at Hamilton Field, California.

On the morning of August 30, 1947, [REDACTED] delivered the negatives to the Phoenix Office at which time they were accepted with the understanding they were being given to Mr. [REDACTED], a representative of the Army Air Force Intelligence, United States Army, and that there was little, if any, chance of his getting the negatives back. Mr. [REDACTED] turned the negatives over to the FBI with the full understanding they were being given to the Air Force and he would not get them back.
